title: 1. Getting started with RefBasedMI
text: longitudinal clinical trial, patients deviate protocol (example discontinuing randomised treatment) often missing outcomes afterwards. primary analysis “missing random” (MAR) assumes patients continued along arm’s trajectory. Reference-based multiple imputation instead encodes assumptions deviating patients resemble reference arm — typically control — usually plausible conservative active treatment. RefBasedMI implements methods, following Carpenter, Roger Kenward (2013) causal model White, Royes Best (2020). R port Stata program mimix. reference-based methods differ post-deviation mean constructed:

title: 1. Getting started with RefBasedMI
text: bundled asthma trial outcome fev measured weeks 2, 4, 8 12, baseline covariate base. impute jump--reference control (arm 1) reference, creating M = 5 completed datasets. result long format: original data (.imp = 0) stacked M imputed datasets (.imp = 1, ..., M). Observed values unchanged; post-deviation missing values filled .
code: imp <- impute(   data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,   covar = base, method = "J2R", reference = 1, M = 5, seed = 101 ) dim(imp) #> [1] 4392    6 head(imp[, c(".imp", "id", "treat", "time", "fev")]) #>    .imp   id treat time  fev #> 7     0 5001     1    2 2.87 #> 18    0 5001     1    4 2.66 #> 23    0 5001     1    8 2.69 #> 3     0 5001     1   12 2.58 #> 34    0 5003     2    2 2.61 #> 41    0 5003     2    4 2.45

title: 1. Getting started with RefBasedMI
text: output designed turned mids object mice package pooled:
code: fit <- with(mice::as.mids(imp), lm(fev ~ factor(treat), subset = (time == 12))) summary(mice::pool(fit)) #>             term  estimate std.error statistic       df      p.value #> 1    (Intercept) 1.8864398 0.1048109 17.998506 17.77053 7.464593e-13 #> 2 factor(treat)2 0.2239927 0.1198322  1.869219 97.35187 6.459826e-02

title: 1. Getting started with RefBasedMI
text: methods differ assumption deviating patients, instructive compare imputed week-12 means across methods using seed. reference-based methods (J2R, CIR, CR) generally pull active arm’s imputed outcomes toward control arm relative MAR.
code: # RefBasedMI captures `method` by non-standard evaluation, so splice the literal # method string into the call with bquote() rather than passing a loop variable. impute_method <- function(m) {   cl <- bquote(RefBasedMI(     data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,     covar = base, method = .(m), reference = 1, M = 5, seed = 101   ))   out <- NULL   utils::capture.output(suppressMessages(out <- eval(cl)))   out }  methods <- c("MAR", "J2R", "CIR", "CR") wk12 <- sapply(methods, function(m) {   out <- impute_method(m)   mean(out$fev[out$.imp > 0 & out$time == 12 & out$treat == 2]) }) round(wk12, 3) #>   MAR   J2R   CIR    CR  #> 2.208 2.110 2.166 2.182

title: 1. Getting started with RefBasedMI
text: causal model interpolates J2R CIR. maintained treatment effect deviation fraction K0 last -treatment effect, decaying geometrically K1 period: K0 = 1, K1 = 0 reproduces J2R K0 = 1, K1 = 1 reproduces CIR.
code: acu <- impute(   data = acupuncture, depvar = head, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,   covar = head_base, method = "Causal", reference = 1, K0 = 1, K1 = 0.5,   M = 5, seed = 101 ) dim(acu) #> [1] 4812   12

title: 1. Getting started with RefBasedMI
text: Delta adjustment adds user-specified increment post-deviation imputations (interim missing values), tipping-point sensitivity analyses. delta gives per-period increments dlag weights . constant shift -1 first post-deviation visit, accruing thereafter, :
code: impD <- impute(   data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,   covar = base, method = "J2R", reference = 2,   delta = c(-1, 0, 0, 0), dlag = c(1, 1, 1, 1), M = 5, seed = 101 ) dim(impD) #> [1] 4392    6

title: 1. Getting started with RefBasedMI
text: missing value followed later observed value treated interim imputed MAR regardless chosen reference-based method; post-discontinuation (monotone) missingness uses reference-based assumption. matches mimix behaviour requires special handling user.

title: 1. Getting started with RefBasedMI
text: Seed. seed defaults NULL, imputations random run. Pass integer reproducibility. Baseline. Supply baseline outcome covariate (covar) rather outcome value, treatment effect assumed baseline. MCMC settings. burnin controls number burn-iterations fitting multivariate normal model; default generally adequate. Increase M stable pooled inference. Individual-specific methods. Assigning different method per patient via methodvar/referencevar supported release. vary method across subgroups, impute subgroup separately combine results.

title: 1. Getting started with RefBasedMI
text: Carpenter J R, Roger J H, Kenward M G (2013). Analysis longitudinal trials protocol deviation. Journal Biopharmaceutical Statistics 23(6), 1352–1371. White R, Royes J, Best N (2020). causal modelling framework reference-based imputation tipping point analysis. Journal Biopharmaceutical Statistics 30(2), 334–350.

title: 3. Sensitivity analysis: the causal model and delta adjustment
text: causal model White, Royes Best (2020) retains, lag k discontinuation, fraction K0 * K1^k last -treatment effect. two constants clear interpretation: K0 fraction treatment effect retained immediately discontinuation; K1 (0 1) per-period decay retained effect. limits recover standard methods: K0 = 1, K1 = 0 J2R (effect vanishes immediately) K0 = 1, K1 = 1 CIR (effect fully retained).  K1 increases 0 1, imputed active-arm outcome rises J2R value toward CIR value, tracing continuum maintained effect.
code: causal_run <- function(K1) {   cl <- bquote(RefBasedMI(     data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,     covar = base, method = "Causal", reference = 1, K0 = 1, K1 = .(K1),     M = 10, seed = 2024   ))   quiet(eval(cl)) }  K1_grid <- c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1) wk12 <- sapply(K1_grid, function(k) {   out <- causal_run(k)   d <- out[out$.imp > 0 & out$time == 12 & out$treat == 2, ]   mean(d$fev) }) names(wk12) <- paste0("K1=", K1_grid) round(wk12, 3) #>    K1=0 K1=0.25  K1=0.5 K1=0.75    K1=1  #>   2.117   2.117   2.119   2.129   2.177 plot(K1_grid, wk12, type = "b", pch = 19, xlab = expression(K[1]),      ylab = "mean imputed week-12 FEV (active arm)",      main = "Causal model: J2R (K1=0) to CIR (K1=1)")

title: 3. Sensitivity analysis: the causal model and delta adjustment
text: Delta adjustment adds fixed offset every post-discontinuation imputed value. making offset progressively unfavourable active arm, can find point significant treatment effect overturned – tipping-point analysis. Reading table, estimated active-versus-reference difference week 12 shrinks (p-value grows) delta penalty increases, showing robust primary conclusion unfavourable departures J2R.
code: treatment_effect <- function(delta_value) {   out <- quiet(RefBasedMI(     data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,     covar = base, method = "J2R", reference = 2,     delta = c(delta_value, delta_value, delta_value, delta_value),     dlag = c(1, 1, 1, 1), M = 10, seed = 2024   ))   fit <- with(mice::as.mids(out),               lm(fev ~ factor(treat) + base, subset = (time == 12)))   est <- summary(mice::pool(fit))   est[est$term == "factor(treat)2", c("estimate", "p.value")] }  deltas <- c(0, -0.25, -0.5, -0.75, -1) tip <- do.call(rbind, lapply(deltas, treatment_effect)) tip <- cbind(delta = deltas, tip) round(tip, 4) #>    delta estimate p.value #> 2   0.00   0.1206  0.2239 #> 21 -0.25   0.3260  0.0029 #> 22 -0.50   0.5314  0.0000 #> 23 -0.75   0.7369  0.0000 #> 24 -1.00   0.9423  0.0000

title: 3. Sensitivity analysis: the causal model and delta adjustment
text: missing value followed later observed value interim rather post-discontinuation. RefBasedMI always imputes interim values MAR, regardless chosen reference-based method; monotone, post- discontinuation missingness uses reference-based assumption. matches behaviour Stata mimix program needs special handling.

title: 3. Sensitivity analysis: the causal model and delta adjustment
text: White R, Royes J, Best N (2020). causal modelling framework reference-based imputation tipping point analysis. Journal Biopharmaceutical Statistics 30(2), 334–350.

title: 2. The reference-based imputation methods
text: patient discontinues treatment, methods differ mean conditional distribution post-deviation values drawn: MAR least conservative (treatment benefit assumed persist); CR J2R conservative effective experimental arm.

title: 2. The reference-based imputation methods
code: obs <- stats::aggregate(fev ~ treat + time, data = asthma, FUN = mean) plot(NULL, xlim = c(2, 12), ylim = range(obs$fev), xlab = "week",      ylab = "mean FEV (observed)", main = "Observed means by arm") for (a in sort(unique(obs$treat))) {   d <- obs[obs$treat == a, ]   lines(d$time, d$fev, type = "b", lty = a, pch = 19, col = a) } legend("topright", legend = paste("arm", sort(unique(obs$treat))),        lty = sort(unique(obs$treat)), col = sort(unique(obs$treat)), pch = 19)

title: 2. The reference-based imputation methods
text: impute active arm (arm 2) method, arm 1 reference, plot imputed mean trajectory (averaged 20 imputations).  missingness asthma late dropout, methods agree early visits fan week 12: MAR keeps active arm highest, CR J2R pull toward (lower) reference arm.
code: methods <- c("MAR", "J2R", "CR", "CIR", "LMCF") fits <- lapply(methods, function(m) arm_means(impute_method(m))) names(fits) <- methods  active <- lapply(fits, function(f) f[f$treat == 2, ]) yr <- range(unlist(lapply(active, function(d) d$fev))) plot(NULL, xlim = c(2, 12), ylim = yr, xlab = "week",      ylab = "mean imputed FEV, active arm",      main = "Active-arm mean by method (reference = arm 1)") for (i in seq_along(active)) {   d <- active[[i]]   lines(d$time, d$fev, type = "b", pch = 19, col = i, lty = i) } legend("bottomleft", legend = methods, col = seq_along(methods),        lty = seq_along(methods), pch = 19, bty = "n")

what: Numerical summary at the final visit
title: 2. The reference-based imputation methods
code: wk12 <- sapply(methods, function(m) {   f <- fits[[m]]   f$fev[f$treat == 2 & f$time == 12] }) round(wk12, 3) #>   MAR   J2R    CR   CIR  LMCF  #> 2.211 2.117 2.181 2.177 2.214

title: 2. The reference-based imputation methods
text: Use MAR primary analysis persisting treatment effect discontinuation defensible. J2R CR encode immediate loss benefit common conservative primary sensitivity analyses superiority trials. CIR intermediate: benefit already accrued retained benefit gained. LMCF rarely primary analysis useful comparison. causal model provides continuum J2R CIR; see vignette("causal--delta").

title: Reference-Based Imputation for Longitudinal Clinical Trials with Protocol Deviation
text: RefBasedMI ports functionality Stata program mimix R adds Causal model Delta adjustment. imputes missing numerical outcomes longitudinal clinical trials treatment-arm-based assumptions unobserved data, following algorithm Carpenter, Roger Kenward (2013) causal model White, Royes Best (2020). mimix program described : Cro S, Morris T P, Kenward M G, Carpenter J R. Reference-based sensitivity analysis via multiple imputation longitudinal trials protocol deviation. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5796638/

title: Reference-Based Imputation for Longitudinal Clinical Trials with Protocol Deviation
text: five reference-based methods : Causal model, treatment effect maintained discontinuation related final -treatment effect: constant fraction K0, decaying geometrically factor K1 (0 <= K1 <= 1) period. K0 = 1, K1 = 0 equivalent J2R; K0 = 1, K1 = 1 equivalent CIR. See White, Royes Best (2020), https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/10543406.2019.1684308. Delta adjustment, user-specified increment added values imputed discontinuation (interim missing values), probe departures primary assumption. increment time k patient discontinued time p delta[p+1]*dlag[1] + delta[p+2]*dlag[2] + ... + delta[k]*dlag[k-p]. common increment 3 every post-discontinuation time obtained delta = c(3, 3, 3, ...) dlag = c(1, 0, 0, ...). See James Roger’s SAS “five macros” https://www.lshtm.ac.uk/research/centres-projects-groups/missing-data#dia-working-group.

title: Reference-Based Imputation for Longitudinal Clinical Trials with Protocol Deviation
text: seed defaults NULL (imputations random run); pass integer reproducibility.
code: library(RefBasedMI)  # Jump-to-reference imputation of the asthma trial, reference arm 1 asthmaJ2R <- RefBasedMI(   data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat, idvar = id, timevar = time,   covar = base, method = "J2R", reference = 1, M = 5, seed = 54321 )

code: library(mice) fit <- with(as.mids(asthmaJ2R), lm(fev ~ factor(treat), subset = (time == 12))) summary(pool(fit))

what: RefBasedMI: Reference-Based Imputation for Longitudinal Clinical Trials with Protocol Deviation — RefBasedMI-package
title: RefBasedMI: Reference-Based Imputation for Longitudinal Clinical Trials with Protocol Deviation — RefBasedMI-package
text: Imputation missing numerical outcomes longitudinal trial protocol deviations. package uses distinct treatment arm-based assumptions unobserved data, following general algorithm Carpenter, Roger, Kenward (2013) doi:10.1080/10543406.2013.834911 , causal model White, Royes Best (2020) doi:10.1080/10543406.2019.1684308 . Sensitivity analyses departures assumptions can done Delta method Roger. program uses algorithm 'mimix' 'Stata' package written Suzie Cro, additional coding causal model delta method. reference-based methods jump reference (J2R), copy increments reference (CIR), copy reference (CR), causal model, must specify reference treatment arm. methods missing random (MAR) last mean carried forward (LMCF).

title: RefBasedMI: Reference-Based Imputation for Longitudinal Clinical Trials with Protocol Deviation — RefBasedMI-package
text: Maintainer: Matteo Quartagno m.quartagno@ucl.ac.uk Authors: Kevin McGrath kkevinmcgrath@yahoo.co.uk Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi .sofimahmudi@gmail.com (ORCID)

what: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
text: RefBasedMI() performs reference-based multiple imputation continuous longitudinal outcome missing treatment discontinuation randomised clinical trial. implements controlled multiple imputation methods Carpenter, Roger Kenward (2013) – missing random (MAR), jump reference (J2R), copy reference (CR), copy increments reference (CIR) last mean carried forward (LMCF) – together causal model White, Royes Best (2020) delta adjustment sensitivity analysis. R port Stata program mimix Suzie Cro.

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
code: RefBasedMI(   data,   covar = NULL,   depvar,   treatvar,   idvar,   timevar,   method = NULL,   reference = NULL,   methodvar = NULL,   referencevar = NULL,   K0 = NULL,   K1 = NULL,   delta = NULL,   dlag = NULL,   M = 1,   seed = NULL,   prior = "jeffreys",   burnin = 1000,   bbetween = NULL,   mle = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
text: data Data frame trial data long format (one row per participant-time), sorted participant. covar Baseline covariate(s) given name character vector; must complete (missing values) numeric factor. Typically baseline outcome. depvar Continuous outcome variable imputed. treatvar Treatment arm variable; numeric character. idvar Participant identifier variable. timevar Variable giving repeated-measures time point. method Group-level imputation method: one "MAR", "J2R", "CR", "CIR", "LMCF" "Causal" (case-insensitive). Exactly one method methodvar must supplied. reference Reference arm "J2R", "CIR", "CR" control arm "Causal"; numeric character matching level treatvar. Required methods. methodvar Variable specifying per-participant method. Individual-specific methods supported release (see Details); supplying raises error. referencevar Variable specifying per-participant reference arm. supported release (see methodvar). K0 Causal model constant (maintained fraction treatment effect); used method = "Causal". K1 Causal model decay constant [0, 1]; maintained effect decays K1 period. Used method = "Causal". delta Optional numeric vector delta increments (values Roger's "five macros"), length equal number time points. dlag Optional numeric vector delta lag weights (b values), length equal number time points; defaults ones. M Number imputations create. seed Optional integer seed. Supply value reproducible imputations; NULL (default) imputations random run. prior Prior multivariate normal fit: "jeffreys" (default), "uniform" "ridge". burnin Number MCMC burn-iterations. bbetween Number MCMC iterations successive imputed datasets (defaults burnin). mle Logical; TRUE, impute improperly drawing maximum likelihood estimates instead posterior draw. Use extreme caution: ignores parameter uncertainty invalidates Rubin's-rules intervals. verbose Logical; TRUE (default) function reports progress, including missing-data pattern summary per-pattern imputation messages. Set FALSE run silently, convenient calling RefBasedMI() repeatedly, example sensitivity grid.

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
text: data frame long format stacking original data (.imp = 0) M imputed datasets (.imp = 1, ..., M). .imp column identifies imputation participant identifier retained. Observed values unchanged; post-discontinuation (interim) missing outcomes filled . Pass result mice::.mids() analyse Rubin's rules.

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
text: Patients deviate protocol (commonly discontinuing randomised treatment) often unobserved post-deviation outcomes. analysis must state outcomes assumed . standard MAR analysis assumes deviating patient continued along arm's trajectory. Reference-based methods instead assume patient comes resemble reference arm (usually control), frequently plausible conservative experimental treatment.

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
text: M imputations routine: builds summary table treatment arm missing-data pattern; fits multivariate normal distribution arm MCMC (bundled norm2 engine); imputes interim (non-monotone) missing values MAR; imputes post-discontinuation (monotone) missing values chosen method, constructing patient's conditional mean relevant arm reference-arm means; applies delta adjustment requested. M completed datasets stacked original data one long data frame analysis Rubin's rules.

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
text: Writing patient's -arm reference-arm mean trajectories \(\mu\) \(\mu^{ref}\), post-discontinuation conditional mean : "MAR" -arm mean \(\mu\); reference required. "J2R" jumps reference mean \(\mu^{ref}\) first missing visit. "CR" follows reference mean \(\mu^{ref}\) throughout. "CIR" retains treatment increment achieved deviation, accrues reference-arm increments thereafter. "LMCF" carries last observed mean forward; reference required. "Causal" maintains fraction last -treatment effect decays geometrically: effect lag \(k\) multiplied \(K0 \times K1^{k}\). K0 = 1, K1 = 0 reproduces J2R K0 = 1, K1 = 1 reproduces CIR.

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
text: Delta adjustment adds increment post-discontinuation imputed value (interim missing values). patient discontinued time \(p\), increment time \(k\) \(\delta_{p+1} d_1 + \delta_{p+2} d_2 + \dots + \delta_k d_{k-p}\), \(\delta\) = delta \(d\) = dlag.

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
text: Supply baseline outcome covariate (covar) rather outcome value, treatment effect assumed baseline. Convert output mice::.mids() analyse Rubin's rules. Individual-specific imputation methods (methodvar referencevar) supported release: code path can leave records unimputed reproduce corresponding group-level imputations, raises error rather returning partial results. vary method across subgroups, call RefBasedMI() separately subgroup combine results.

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI
text: Carpenter J R, Roger J H, Kenward M G (2013). Analysis longitudinal trials protocol deviation: framework relevant, accessible assumptions, inference via multiple imputation. Journal Biopharmaceutical Statistics 23(6), 1352–1371. doi:10.1080/10543406.2013.834911 White R, Royes J, Best N (2020). causal modelling framework reference-based imputation tipping point analysis clinical trials quantitative outcome. Journal Biopharmaceutical Statistics 30(2), 334–350. doi:10.1080/10543406.2019.1684308

title: Reference-based multiple imputation of longitudinal clinical trial data — RefBasedMI

"J2R", reference = 1,   M = 2, seed = 54321) #>     #>  #> Summary of missing data pattern by treat: #>  #>    pattern treat patients fev.2.miss fev.4.miss fev.8.miss fev.12.miss #> 1        0     1 #> 4       12     1       22          0          0          1           1 #> 5       13     1        1          1          0          1           1 #> 6       14     1       16          0          1 mcmcNorm Loop finished. #>  #>  #> Number of original missing values = 147 #>  #> Imputing interim missing values under MAR: #>  #> treat = 1pattern = 7number patients = 1 #> treat = 1pattern = =  1 pattern =  0 number patients =  38  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  8 number patients =  15  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  12 number patients =  23  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  14 number patients =  16  #> timevar = time, covar = base, method = "MAR", M = 2, seed = 54321) #>     #>  #> Summary of missing data pattern by treat: #>  #>    pattern treat patients fev.2.miss fev.4.miss fev.8.miss fev.12.miss 1 to 2 #>  #> mcmcNorm Loop finished. #>  #>  #> Number of original missing values = 147 #>  #> Imputing interim missing values under MAR: #>  #> treat = 1pattern = 7number patients = 1 #> treat = 1pattern = 13number patients = 1 #> treat = 2pattern = 4number patients = 1 #>  #> Number of post-discontinuation missing values = 142 #>  #> Imputing post-discontinuation missing values under MAR: #> =  16  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  0 number patients =  72  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  8 number patients =  8  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  12 number patients =  8  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  14 number patients =  3  #>  #> Number of final missing values = 0 #> End of RefBasedMI  # Copy increments in reference asthmaCIR <- RefBasedMI(data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat,   idvar = id, timevar = time, covar = base, method = "CIR", reference = 1,   M = 2, seed = 54321) #>     #>  #> Summary of missing data pattern by treat: #>  #>    pattern treat patients fev.2.miss fev.4.miss fev.8.miss fev.12.miss #> 1        0     1       37          0          0          0           0 #> 2        7     1        1          1          1          1           0 #> 3        8     1       15          0          0          0           1 #> 4       12     1       22          0          0          1           1 #> 5       13     1        1          1          0          1           1 #> 6       14     1       16          0          1          1           1 #> 7        0     2       71          0          0          0           0 #> 8        4     2        1          0          0          1           0 #> 9        8     2        8          0          0          0           1 #> 10      12     2        8          0          0          1           1 #> 11      14     2        3          0          1          1           1 #>  #> Fitting multivariate normal model by treat: #>   #>  #> treat = 1 #> performing mcmcNorm for m = 1 to 2 #>  #> mcmcNorm Loop finished. #>  #> treat = 2 #> performing mcmcNorm for m = 1 to 2 #>  #> mcmcNorm Loop finished. #>  #>  #> Number of original missing values = 147 #>  #> Imputing interim missing values under MAR: #>  #> treat = 1pattern = 7number patients = 1 #> treat = 1pattern = 13number patients = 1 #> treat = 2pattern = 4number patients = 1 #>  #> Number of post-discontinuation missing values = 142 #>  #> Imputing post-discontinuation missing values under CIR: #>  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  0 number patients =  38  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  8 number patients =  15  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  12 number patients =  23  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  14 number patients =  16  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  0 number patients =  72  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  8 number patients =  8  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  12 number patients =  8  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  14 number patients =  3  #>  #> Number of final missing values = 0 #> End of RefBasedMI  # Causal model: half the treatment effect retained, decaying by 0.5 per period asthmaCausal <- RefBasedMI(data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat,   idvar = id, timevar = time, covar = base, method = "Causal", reference = 1,   K0 = 1, K1 = 0.5, M = 2, seed = 54321) #>     #>  #> Summary of missing data pattern by treat: #>  #>    pattern treat patients fev.2.miss fev.4.miss fev.8.miss fev.12.miss #> 1        0     1       37          0          0          0           0 #> 2        7     1        1          1          1          1           0 #> 3        8     1       15          0          0          0           1 #> 4       12     1       22          0          0          1           1 #> 5       13     1        1          1          0          1           1 #> 6       14     1       16          0          1          1           1 #> 7        0     2       71          0          0          0           0 #> 8        4     2        1          0          0          1           0 #> 9        8     2        8          0          0          0           1 #> 10      12     2        8          0          0          1           1 #> 11      14     2        3          0          1          1           1 #>  #> Fitting multivariate normal model by treat: #>   #>  #> treat = 1 #> performing mcmcNorm for m = 1 to 2 #>  #> mcmcNorm Loop finished. #>  #> treat = 2 #> performing mcmcNorm for m = 1 to 2 #>  #> mcmcNorm Loop finished. #>  #>  #> Number of original missing values = 147 #>  #> Imputing interim missing values under MAR: #>  #> treat = 1pattern = 7number patients = 1 #> treat = 1pattern = 13number patients = 1 #> treat = 2pattern = 4number patients = 1 #>  #> Number of post-discontinuation missing values = 142 #>  #> Imputing post-discontinuation missing values under CAUSAL: #>  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  0 number patients =  38  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  8 number patients =  15  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  12 number patients =  23  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  14 number patients =  16  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  0 number patients =  72  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  8 number patients =  8  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  12 number patients =  8  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  14 number patients =  3  #>  #> Number of final missing values = 0 #> End of RefBasedMI  # Delta adjustment: shift post-discontinuation imputations down by 1 unit asthmaDelta <- RefBasedMI(data = asthma, depvar = fev, treatvar = treat,   idvar = id, timevar = time, covar = base, method = "J2R", reference = 2,   delta = c(-1, 0, 0, 0), dlag = c(1, 1, 1, 1), M = 2, seed = 54321) #>     #>  #> Summary of missing data pattern by treat: #>  #>    pattern treat patients fev.2.miss fev.4.miss fev.8.miss fev.12.miss #> 1        0     1       37          0          0          0           0 #> 2        7     1        1          1          1          1           0 #> 3        8     1       15 patients = 1 #> treat = 1pattern = 13number patients = 1 #> treat = 2pattern = 4number patients = 1 #>  #> Number of post-discontinuation missing values = 142 #>  #> Imputing post-discontinuation missing values under J2R: #>  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  0 number patients =  38  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  8 number patients =  15  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  12 number patients =  23  #> treat  =  1 pattern =  14 number patients =  16  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  0 number patients =  72  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  8 number patients =  8  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  12 number patients =  8  #> treat  =  2 pattern =  14 number patients =  3  #>  #> Number of final missing values = 0 #> End of RefBasedMI  # Analyse the imputations with Rubin's rules via the mice package # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("mice", quietly = TRUE)) {   fit <- with(mice::as.mids(asthmaJ2R),               lm(fev ~ factor(treat), subset = (time == 12)))   summary(mice::pool(fit)) } #>             term  estimate  std.error statistic       df      p.value #> 1    (Intercept) 1.8484905 0.09751412  18.95613 11.77294 3.480994e-10 #> 2 factor(treat)2 0.2586256 0.12345074   2.09497 66.75392 3.997138e-02 # }

title: Acupuncture trial data — acupuncture
text: Data randomised, double-blind, parallel-group trial comparing active treatment placebo. outcome head (headache severity score) recorded long format times 3 12.

title: Acupuncture trial data — acupuncture
code: acupuncture

title: Acupuncture trial data — acupuncture
text: data frame 802 rows 11 variables: id patient identifier time measurement time point (3 12) age patient age, years sex patient sex migraine indicator migraine (versus tension-type headache) chronicity duration headache disorder, years practice_id recruiting practice identifier treat randomised treatment arm (1 2) head_base baseline headache score, used covariate head headache severity score, outcome (missing discontinuation) withdrawal_reason reason withdrawal, recorded

title: Acupuncture trial data — acupuncture
text: Vickers J et al. acupuncture chronic headache trial.

title: Antidepressant trial data — antidepressant
text: antidepressant clinical trial data described White, Royes Best (2020). outcome HAMD17.TOTAL (Hamilton depression rating) recorded long format visits 4, 5, 6 7. data also carry per-patient method reference columns used illustrate individual-specific imputation.

title: Antidepressant trial data — antidepressant
code: antidepressant

title: Antidepressant trial data — antidepressant
text: data frame 688 rows 14 variables: PATIENT.NUMBER patient identifier HAMA.TOTAL Hamilton anxiety rating scale total PGI.IMPROVEMENT patient global impression improvement VISIT.DATE day visit relative randomisation VISIT.NUMBER visit number (4, 5, 6, 7) TREATMENT.NAME randomised treatment arm PATIENT.SEX patient sex POOLED.INVESTIGATOR pooled investigator centre basval baseline value outcome, used covariate HAMD17.TOTAL Hamilton 17-item depression rating, outcome change change baseline outcome miss_flag indicator record observed methodcol per-patient imputation method (individual-specific imputation) referencecol per-patient reference arm (individual-specific imputation)

title: Asthma trial data — asthma
text: asthma clinical trial data used Stata mimix help file. outcome fev (forced expiratory volume) recorded long format weeks 2, 4, 8 12, post-baseline values missing.

title: Asthma trial data — asthma
code: asthma

title: Asthma trial data — asthma
text: data frame 732 rows 5 variables: id patient identifier time measurement time point, weeks (2, 4, 8, 12) treat randomised treatment arm (1 2) base baseline value outcome, used covariate fev forced expiratory volume, outcome (missing discontinuation)

title: Asthma trial data — asthma
text: Distributed Stata mimix package Suzie Cro.

title: RefBasedMI 0.3.0
text: maintenance reliability release. reference-based imputation results supported group-level methods (MAR, J2R, CR, CIR, LMCF, Causal) unchanged: every change verified 14-scenario regression baseline captured version 0.2.0, scenarios remain numerically identical unless noted.

title: RefBasedMI 0.3.0
text: seed now defaults NULL instead 101. Imputations random default reproducible seed supplied. Code relied implicit default must now pass seed = 101 explicitly. Individual-specific imputation via methodvar / referencevar now raises explicit error. feature non-functional previous versions (leave records unimputed reproduce group-level imputations), disabled rather allowed return partial results. vary method across subgroups, impute subgroup separately group-level method reference arguments combine results.

title: RefBasedMI 0.3.0
text: Fixed native routine registration: C initialisation routine misnamed (copy-paste leftover) registered routines, leaving package reliant dynamic symbol lookup. five Fortran routines bundled norm2 engine now properly registered. loglikNorm() dispatched wrong S3 generic computed log-posterior instead log-likelihood; corrected. Treatment- id-column handling used substring matching (grep) boundary-unsafe index arithmetic; replaced exact matching position-safe permutation. Corrected delta guard length(delta != 0) replaced 1:length(x) loop bounds seq_along() vector can empty. Repaired several latent errors individual-methods entry path (short-circuit logical bug, missing non-standard-evaluation referencevar, data-frame indexing error causal helper) ahead gate .

title: RefBasedMI 0.3.0
text: Reduced runtime dependencies base R (stats, utils). Removed Hmisc (replaced internal, order-identical grouping helper), data.table, pastecs, assertthat. Moved mice Suggests (used examples vignettes).

title: RefBasedMI 0.3.0
text: Rewrote reference manual: structured RefBasedMI() help page per-method descriptions, delta formula, complete parameter documentation, references, worked examples; complete dataset documentation. Added three vignettes: getting-started guide, methods comparison imputed-trajectory plots, sensitivity-analysis vignette covering causal model delta tipping-point analysis. Added testthat suite, GitHub Actions R CMD check lintr, pkgdown website. Added Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi author.
